channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Baby chicks link certain sounds with shapes, just like humans do
item.description: A surprising new study shows that baby chickens react the same way that humans do when tested for something called the "bouba-kiki effect," which has ...

item.title: Michael Pollan says AI may 'think' — but it will never be conscious
item.description: "Consciousness is under siege," says author Michael Pollan. His new book, A World Appears, explores consciousness on both a personal and technological...

item.title: The U.S. and Canada set to square off in Olympic women's ice hockey gold medal match
item.description: Canada was long the top dog of Olympic women's hockey. But with a win Thursday, the Americans could do more than earn a third gold medal — they could ...

item.title: American Jordan Stolz speedskates to a third Olympic medal -- silver this time
item.description: U.S. speedskater Jordan Stolz had a lot of hype accompanying him in these Winter Olympic Games. He's now got two gold medals, one silver, with one eve...

item.title: Reporter's notebook: My Olympic Lunar New Year
item.description: An NPR reporter covering the Olympics in Milan takes us on cultural side quests, to a hospitality house and a candy store.

item.title: Off by Nearly 1 MILLION Jobs? Why New Jobs Report Will Impact Rentals
item.description: Big economic news dropped last week: labor data, inflation rates, and huge jobs revisions. All of these are already impacting the housing market, but ...

item.title: Dangerous conditions delay the recovery of 8 killed in California avalanche
item.description: Authorities in California say it will be at least another day before crews can attempt to recover the bodies of eight people killed in an avalanche an...

item.title: Why these startup CEOs don’t think AI will replace human roles
item.description: The CEOs of Read AI and Lucidya told TechCrunch at Web Summit Qatar that they see AI tools replacing tasks, rather than workers.

item.title: SpaceX’s Starbase city is getting its own court
item.description: Elon Musk's company town already has a volunteer fire department, and is forming a Starbase Police Department. Now it wants its own court, too.

item.title: YouTube’s latest experiment brings its conversational AI tool to TVs
item.description: YouTube is testing conversational AI on smart TVs, allowing viewers to ask the assistant questions related to the video they're watching on the big sc...

item.title: Toyota hires seven Agility humanoid robots for Canadian factory
item.description: The robots will be unloading totes full of auto parts from an automated warehouse tugger.

item.title: When Rhode Island shooter started firing, bystanders jumped into action to end the carnage
The boys’ club no one was supposed to write about
item.description: Reporter Zoë Bernard spent months talking to 51 people (31 of them gay men) to map out a subculture that's been an open secret in Silicon Valley for y...

item.title: Snow drought helped set the stage for deadly California avalanche, leading to unstable conditions
The Housing For the 21st Century Act Brings Game Changers For Multifamily Investors
item.description: There’s nothing that unifies opposing political factions faster than seeing homeless encampments in American cities. That’s why the Housing for the 21...

item.title: Single-Family vs. Multifamily Rentals: Which Is the Best First Rental?
item.description: Single-family vs. multifamily rental properties—which gets you to financial freedom faster? A rookie real estate investor is wondering what he should ...

item.title: How to Buy Your First (or Next) Rental Property in 2026 (Step by Step)
item.description: Want to buy your first rental property in 2026? You’ve come to the right place! Whether you dream of becoming a “small and mighty” investor or buildin...

item.title: How the Wealthiest Have Programmed Their Portfolios This Year
item.description: Despite stock markets hovering around record highs, investors are feeling jittery. You can see it in consumer confidence collapsing to its lowest leve...
